heading: The Next Generation of Image Formats
text: The web has relied on JPEG and PNG for decades, but these formats were designed in an era when bandwidth was expensive and processing power was limited. Modern formats like WebP and AVIF use advanced compression techniques that deliver the same visual quality at a fraction of the file size.

heading: Compression Quality Comparison
text: In controlled comparisons, AVIF consistently outperforms WebP in compression efficiency. At equivalent visual quality, AVIF files are typically 20-30% smaller than WebP files and 50% smaller than JPEG. This advantage is most pronounced at lower bitrates, where AVIF maintains cleaner details and fewer artifacts.

heading: Browser Support and Adoption
text: WebP has near-universal browser support in 2026, covering over 97% of global web users. Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and all major mobile browsers handle WebP without issues. This mature support makes WebP a safe, production-ready choice for any web project.

heading: Which Format Should You Use?
text: Use AVIF as your primary format when targeting modern browsers and maximum compression is a priority. It is the best choice for photography-heavy websites, media galleries, and any project where bandwidth savings directly impact costs or user experience. The compression advantage over WebP translates into measurably faster page loads.
